00106| An :dt:`implicitly declared entity` is an :t:`entity` that lacks an explicit

00116| .. _fls_jdknpu3kf865:
00117| 
00118| Visibility
00119| ----------

00121| .. rubric:: Syntax
00122| 
00123| .. syntax::
00124| 
00125|    VisibilityModifier ::=
00126|        CratePublicModifier
00127|      | SelfPublicModifier
00128|      | SimplePathPublicModifier
00129|      | SimplePublicModifier
00130|      | SuperPublicModifier
00131| 
00132|    CratePublicModifier ::=
00133|        $$pub$$ $$($$ $$crate$$ $$)$$
00134| 
00135|    SelfPublicModifier ::=
00136|        $$pub$$ $$($$ $$self$$ $$)$$
00137| 
00138|    SimplePathPublicModifier ::=
00139|        $$pub$$ $$($$ $$in$$ SimplePath $$)$$
00140| 
00141|    SimplePublicModifier ::=
00142|        $$pub$$
00143| 
00144|    SuperPublicModifier ::=
00145|        $$pub$$ $$($$ $$super$$ $$)$$
00146| 
00147| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00148| 
00149| :dp:`fls_7kpepal8ghuj`
00150| :dt:`Visibility` is a property of :t:`[field]s` and :t:`[item]s` that determines
00151| which :t:`[module]s` can refer to the :dt:`name` of the :t:`field` or :t:`item`.
00152| 
00153| :dp:`fls_qo0itr5il1kk`
00154| :dt:`Public visibility` is a kind of :t:`visibility` that allows for a :t:`name`
00155| to be referred to from arbitrary :t:`module` ``M`` as long as the ancestor
00156| :t:`[module]s` of the related :t:`entity` can be referred to from ``M``.
00157| 
00158| :dp:`fls_knjruq5wppv`
00159| :dt:`Private visibility` is a kind of :t:`visibility` that allows a :t:`name`
00160| to be referred to only by the current :t:`module` of the :t:`entity`, and its
00161| descendant :t:`[module]s`.
00162| 
00163| :dp:`fls_t7i4n19qdgn4`
00164| A :dt:`visibility modifier` sets the :t:`visibility` of a :t:`name`.
00165| 
00166| :dp:`fls_aa4f3rvir9lm`
00167| A :dt:`crate public modifier` is a :t:`visibility modifier` that grants a
00168| :t:`name` :t:`public visibility` within the current :t:`crate` only.
00169| 
00170| :dp:`fls_tnh7o3pb4e22`
00171| A :dt:`self public modifier` is a :t:`visibility modifier` that grants a
00172| :t:`name` :t:`private visibility`. A :t:`self public modifier` is equivalent
00173| to a :t:`simple path public modifier` where the :t:`simple path` denotes
00174| :t:`keyword` ``self``.
00175| 
00176| :dp:`fls_yymgpyi67dty`
00177| A :dt:`simple path public modifier` is a :t:`visibility modifier` that grants a
00178| :t:`name` :t:`public visibility` within the provided :t:`simple path` only.
00179| 
00180| :dp:`fls_hc121mxknq03`
00181| The :t:`simple path` of a :t:`simple path public modifier` shall start
00182| with a :t:`path segment` expressed by either :t:`keyword` ``crate``,
00183| :t:`keyword` ``self``, or :t:`keyword` ``super``.
00184| 
00185| :dp:`fls_icztzxjpm1du`
00186| The :t:`simple path` of a :t:`simple path public modifier` shall resolve to
00187| an ancestor :t:`module` of the current :t:`module` or the current :t:`module`
00188| itself.
00189| 
00190| :dp:`fls_np8aghofjqhm`
00191| A :dt:`simple public modifier` is a :t:`visibility modifier` that grants a
00192| :t:`name` :t:`public visibility`.
00193| 
00194| :dp:`fls_quzvhzpr0124`
00195| A :dt:`super public modifier` is a :t:`visibility modifier` that grants a
00196| :t:`name` :t:`public visibility` within the parent :t:`module` only. A
00197| :t:`super public modifier` is equivalent to a :t:`simple path public modifier`
00198| where the :t:`simple path` denotes :t:`keyword` ``super``.
00199| 
00200| :dp:`fls_utgjx6l5zwfl`
00201| An external :t:`item`, a :t:`field`, or an :t:`item` that appears without a
00202| :t:`visibility modifier` has :t:`private visibility` by default.
00203| 
00204| :dp:`fls_jifg2st5bfd6`
00205| An :t:`associated item` of a :t:`trait` has the same :t:`visibility` as the
00206| :t:`trait`.
00207| 
00208| :dp:`fls_dm0xr424ine1`
00209| An :t:`enum variant` and its :t:`[field]s` have the same :t:`visibility` as the

00368| Destruction
00369| -----------
00370| 
00371| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00372| 
00373| :dp:`fls_e7ucq87s806d`
00374| :dt:`Destruction` is the process of recovering resources associated with a
00375| :t:`value` as it goes out of scope.
00376| 
00377| .. _fls_u2mzjgiwbkz0:
00378| 
00379| Destructors
00380| -----------
00381| 
00382| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
00383| 
00384| :dp:`fls_9m0gszdle0qb`
00385| A :dt:`drop type` is a :t:`type` that implements the :std:`core::ops::Drop`
00386| :t:`trait` or contains a :t:`field` that has a :t:`drop type`.
00387| 
00388| :dp:`fls_4nkzidytpi6`
00389| A :dt:`destructor` is a :t:`function` that is invoked immediately before the
00390| :t:`destruction` of a :t:`value` of a :t:`drop type`.
00391| 
00392| :dp:`fls_wzuwapjqtyyy`
00393| :dt:`Dropping` a :t:`value` is the act of invoking the :t:`destructor` of the
00394| related :t:`type`.
00395| 
00396| :dp:`fls_8U45VMMfCU7F`
00397| A :t:`value` is :dt:`dropped` when its :t:`destructor` is invoked.
00398| 
00399| :dp:`fls_gfvm70iqu1l4`
00400| An :t:`uninitialized` :t:`variable` is not :t:`dropped`.
00401| 
00402| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
00403| 
00404| :dp:`fls_l2xkdjeydqtx`
00405| :t:`Dropping` an :t:`initialized` :t:`variable` proceeds as follows:
00406| 
00407| #. :dp:`fls_bync24y6gp93`
00408|    If the :t:`drop type` implements the :std:`core::ops::Drop` :t:`trait`, then
00409|    ``core::ops::Drop::drop`` of the :t:`drop type` is invoked.
00410| 
00411| #. :dp:`fls_jzancf72i95f`
00412|    If the :t:`drop type` is an :t:`array type`, then its elements are
00413|    :t:`dropped` from the first element to the last element.
00414| 
00415| #. :dp:`fls_gjn2jnsal9gs`
00416|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`closure type`, then all
00417|    :t:`[capture target]s` whose :t:`capture mode` is :t:`by move` are
00418|    :t:`dropped` in unspecified order.
00419| 
00420| #. :dp:`fls_ol2w2292frfi`
00421|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is an :t:`enum type`, then the :t:`[field]s`
00422|    of the active :t:`enum variant` are :t:`dropped` in declaration order.
00423| 
00424| #. :dp:`fls_6ii5o68vuymj`
00425|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`slice type`, then its elements are
00426|    :t:`dropped` from the first element to the last element.
00427| 
00428| #. :dp:`fls_sup43es8ps8r`
00429|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`struct type`, then its
00430|    :t:`[field]s` are :t:`dropped` in declaration order.
00431| 
00432| #. :dp:`fls_y9q0eqr865b3`
00433|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`trait object type`, then the
00434|    :t:`destructor` of the underlying :t:`type` is invoked.
00435| 
00436| #. :dp:`fls_kdqng6eovxns`
00437|    Otherwise, if the :t:`drop type` is a :t:`tuple type`, then its :t:`[field]s`
00438|    are :t:`dropped` in declaration order.
00439| 
00440| #. :dp:`fls_ag249y74jg6c`
00441|    Otherwise, :t:`dropping` has no effect.
00442| 
00443| .. rubric:: Examples
00444| 
00445| .. code-block:: rust
00446| 
00447|    struct PrintOnDrop(&'static str);
00448| 
00449|    impl core::ops::Drop for PrintOnDrop {
00450|        fn drop(&mut self) {
00451|            println!("{}", self.0);
00452|        }
00453|    }

04613|    ElseExpression ::=
04614|        $$else$$ (BlockExpression | IfExpression | IfLetExpression)
04615| 
04616| .. rubric:: Legality Rules
04617| 
04618| :dp:`fls_2i4fbxbbvpf1`
04619| An :dt:`if expression` is an :t:`expression` that evaluates either a
04620| :t:`block expression` or an :t:`else expression` depending on the :t:`value` of
04621| its :t:`subject expression`.
04622| 
04623| :dp:`fls_5azwlk7hav1k`
04624| An :dt:`else expression` is an :t:`expression` that represents either a
04625| :t:`block expression`, an :t:`if expression`, or an :t:`if let expression`.
04626| 
04627| :dp:`fls_r7gzxo16esri`
04628| The :t:`type` of the :t:`subject expression` of an :t:`if expression` shall be
04629| :t:`type` :c:`bool`.
04630| 
04631| :dp:`fls_iv9t4nfs4f6w`
04632| The :t:`type` of an :t:`if expression` is the :t:`type` of its
04633| :t:`block expression`.
04634| 
04635| :dp:`fls_i9sxf2q5jjqt`
04636| The :t:`value` of an :t:`if expression` is the :t:`value` of its
04637| :t:`block expression`.
04638| 
04639| :dp:`fls_1e8qer6bh2f3`
04640| The :t:`type` of an :t:`else expression` is the :t:`type` of its
04641| :t:`block expression`, :t:`if expression`, or :t:`if let expression`.
04642| 
04643| :dp:`fls_p5pjxk5xfcbx`
04644| The :t:`value` of an :t:`else expression` is the :t:`value` of its
04645| :t:`block expression`, :t:`if expression`, or :t:`if let expression`.
04646| 
04647| :dp:`fls_mpq7gicosgkt`
04648| The :t:`type` of an :t:`if expression` and the :t:`type` of an
04649| :t:`else expression` shall be :t:`unifiable`.
04650| 
04651| .. rubric:: Dynamic Semantics
04652| 
04653| :dp:`fls_yhlyzef9h97q`
04654| The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`if expression` proceeds as follows:
04655| 
04656| #. :dp:`fls_w7lq4dkoyuf7`
04657|    The :t:`subject expression` is evaluated.
04658| 
04659| #. :dp:`fls_5udx9zyeg5ga`
04660|    If the :t:`subject expression` evaluated to ``true``, then the
04661|    :t:`block expression` is evaluated.
04662| 
04663| #. :dp:`fls_67l4j48n6p7o`
04664|    If the :t:`subject expression` evaluated to ``false`` and the
04665|    :t:`if expression` has an :t:`else expression`, then the :t:`else expression`
04666|    is evaluated.
04667| 
04668| :dp:`fls_e8gd5lzcaifw`
04669| The :t:`evaluation` of an :t:`else expression` evaluates its
04670| :t:`block expression`, :t:`if expression`, or :t:`if let expression`.
04671| 
04672| .. rubric:: Examples
04673| 
04674| .. code-block:: rust
04675| 
04676|    if age <= 14 {
04677|        println!("child");
04678|    } else if age <= 24 {
04679|        println!("youth");
04680|    } else if age <=64 {
04681|        println!("adult");
04682|    } else {
04683|        println!("senior");
04684|    }
04685|

01106| Character Literals
01107| ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
01108| 
01109| .. rubric:: Syntax

01112|

01115|

01118|      | CharacterLiteralCharacter

01120| 
01121|    AsciiEscape ::=
01122|        $$\0$$
01123|      | $$\"$$
01124|      | $$\'$$
01125|      | $$\t$$
01126|      | $$\n$$
01127|      | $$\r$$
01128|      | $$\\$$
01129|      | $$\x$$ OctalDigit HexadecimalDigit
01130| 
01131| :dp:`fls_TXk2yFGh8at5`
01132| An :dt:`escaped character` is the textual representation for a character with
01133| special meaning. An escaped character consists of character 0x5C (reverse
01134| solidus), followed by the single character encoding of the special meaning
01135| character. For example, ``\t`` is the escaped character for 0x09 (horizontal
01136| tabulation).
01137| 
01138| :dp:`fls_j9q9ton57rvl`
01139| A :ds:`CharacterLiteralCharacter` is any :t:`Unicode` character except
01140| characters 0x09 (horizontal tabulation), 0x0A (new line), 0x0D (carriage
01141| return), 0x27 (apostrophe), and 0x5c (reverse solidus).
01142| 
01143| :dp:`fls_5v9gx22g5wPm`
01144| A :ds:`UnicodeEscape` starts with a ``\u{`` literal, followed by 1 to 6
01145| instances of a :s:`HexadecimalDigit`, inclusive, followed by a ``}`` character.
01146| It can represent any :t:`Unicode` codepoint between U+00000 and U+10FFFF,
01147| inclusive, except :t:`Unicode` surrogate codepoints, which exist between
01148| the range of U+D800 and U+DFFF, inclusive.

01154| character.

01157| The :t:`type` of a :t:`character literal` is :c:`char`.

00804| :t:`Attribute` :dc:`track_caller` allows the :t:`function body` of its
00805| related :t:`function` to obtain a :std:`core::panic::Location` which indicates
00806| the topmost untracked caller that ultimately led to the invocation of the

00816| :t:`implementation`, then the :t:`attribute` applies to all overriding
00817| :t:`[function]s`.

files.before:
.. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT OR Apache-2.0
   SPDX-FileCopyrightText: The Ferrocene Developers

.. default-domain:: spec

.. _fls_hdwwrsyunir:

Program Structure and Compilation
=================================

.. _fls_s35hob3i7lr:

Source Files
------------

.. rubric:: Syntax

.. syntax::

   SourceFile ::=
       ZeroWidthNoBreakSpace?
       Shebang?
       InnerAttributeOrDoc*
       Item*

   ZeroWidthNoBreakSpace ::=
       $$\u{FEFF}$$

   Shebang ::=
       $$#!$$ ~[NewLine]*

   NewLine ::=
       $$\n$$

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_4vicosdeaqmp`
A :dt:`source file` contains the program text consisting of :t:`[inner
attribute]s`, :t:`[inner doc comment]s`, and :t:`[item]s`. The location of a
:t:`source file` is tool defined.

:dp:`fls_ann3cha1xpek`
A :s:`Shebang` does not have an effect on the compilation.

.. _fls_e9hwvqsib5d5:

Modules
-------

.. rubric:: Syntax

.. syntax::

   ModuleDeclaration ::=
       $$unsafe$$? $$mod$$ Name ModuleSpecification

   ModuleSpecification ::=
       InlineModuleSpecification
     | OutlineModuleSpecification

   InlineModuleSpecification ::=
       $${$$
         InnerAttributeOrDoc*
         Item*
       $$}$$

   OutlineModuleSpecification ::=
       $$;$$

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_odd1hj3y1mgu`
A :dt:`module` is a container for zero or more :t:`[item]s`.

:dp:`fls_whgv72emrm47`
The ``unsafe`` :t:`keyword` of a :t:`module` is rejected, but may still
be consumed by :t:`[macro]s`.

:dp:`fls_qypjjpcf8uwq`
An :dt:`inline module` is a :t:`module` with an :s:`InlineModuleSpecification`.

:dp:`fls_cavwpr1ybk37`
An :dt:`outline module` is a :t:`module` with an :s:`OutlineModuleSpecification`.

:dp:`fls_plepew2319g4`
An :t:`outline module` loads a :t:`source file` and considers the text of the
:t:`source file` to be inlined within the context of the :t:`outline module`.

:dp:`fls_1aruwps62c4p`
The location of a :t:`module` :t:`source file` can be specified using
:t:`attribute` :c:`path`.

.. rubric:: Examples

.. code-block:: rust

   #[path = "path/to/module"]
   pub mod module {
   	#![allow(dead_code)]

        struct Struct;
        pub mod other;
   }

.. _fls_maw4u1o8q37u:

Crates
------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_qwghk79ok5h0`
A :dt:`crate` is a unit of compilation and linking that contains a tree of
nested :t:`[module]s`.

:dp:`fls_unxalgMqIr3v`
The :dt:`crate type` of a :t:`crate` is the value of the :t:`attribute`
``crate_type`` of a :t:`crate` or the value of ``--crate-type`` flag passed to
the tool compiling the :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_e7jGvXvTsFpC`
The :t:`crate type` of a :t:`crate` if not specified is ``bin``.

:dp:`fls_kQiJPwb2Hjcc`
A :t:`crate` may be subject to multiple :t:`[crate type]s`, treating each type
as a separate :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_9ub6ks8qrang`
A :t:`binary crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``bin``.

:dp:`fls_OyFwBtDGVimT`
A :t:`binary crate` that is not subject to :t:`attribute` ``no_main`` shall have
a :t:`function` in scope of its :t:`crate root module` under the :t:`name`
``main`` with a :t:`main function signature`.

:dp:`fls_jQqXxPyND1ds`
The :t:`function` in scope of a :t:`binary crate`'s :t:`crate root module` under
the :t:`name` ``main`` with a :t:`main function signature` is the :t:`binary
crate`'s :t:`program entry point`.

:dp:`fls_d9nn4yuiw1ja`
A :dt:`library crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``lib``, ``rlib``,
``staticlib``, ``dylib``, or ``cdylib``.

:dp:`fls_Mf62VqAhoZ3c`
A :dt:`proc-macro crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``proc-macro``.

:dp:`fls_RJJmN4tP7j4m`
A :t:`proc-macro crate` shall not declare :t:`[item]s` in its :t:`crate root
module` with  :t:`public visibility` unless the :t:`item` is a :t:`procedural
macro`.

:dp:`fls_h93C3wfbAoz1`
Only a :t:`proc-macro crate` shall declare :t:`[procedural macro]s`.

.. _fls_gklst7joeo33:

Crate Imports
-------------

.. rubric:: Syntax

.. syntax::

   ExternalCrateImport ::=
       $$extern$$ $$crate$$ CrateIndication Renaming? $$;$$

   CrateIndication ::=
       Identifier
     | $$self$$

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_d0pa807s5d5h`
A :dt:`crate import` specifies a required dependency on an external :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_vfam3wzeAiah`
A :dt:`crate indication` is a :t:`construct` that indicates a :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_2v7k7p4v0xra`
A :t:`renaming` provides an alternative :t:`name` for an existing name.

:dp:`fls_ft860vkz0lkc`
A :t:`crate import` binds an external :t:`crate` to its :t:`crate indication`.

:dp:`fls_k90qtnf8kgu1`
:t:`Crate indication` ``self`` shall require a :t:`renaming`.

:dp:`fls_siv8bl6s2ndu`
A :t:`crate import` with a :t:`renaming` with an :t:`identifier` binds the
external :t:`crate` to a local :t:`name` and introduces the local :t:`name` into
the enclosing :t:`scope`.

:dp:`fls_7vz5n3x6jo1s`
If a :t:`crate import` appears at the :t:`crate root module`, then the
:t:`crate indication` is added to the :t:`external prelude`.

:dp:`fls_3bgpc8m8yk4p`
A :t:`crate indication` shall resolve to an external :t:`crate`. The process of
resolving a :t:`crate indication` to an external :t:`crate` is tool-defined.

.. _fls_5w50kf83oo1u:

Compilation Roots
-----------------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_fhiqvgdamq5`
A :dt:`crate root module` is the root of the nested :t:`module` tree of a
:t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_1NrcosNZJu5V`
A :dt:`crate root` is the :t:`crate root module` of a :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_tk8tl2e0a34`
A tool can define a :t:`crate root module` for a single :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_bsyfxdk3ap1t`
A :dt:`compilation root` is an input to a compilation performed by a tool. A
:t:`crate root module` is a :t:`compilation root`.

.. _fls_u1afezy1ye99:

Conditional Compilation
-----------------------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_9stc6nul6vq9`
:dt:`conditionally-compiled source code` is source code that may or may
not be considered a part of a Rust program depending on
:t:`[configuration predicate]s`.

:dp:`fls_a0u9nnaf6drz`
:t:`conditional compilation` is the process of compiling
:t:`conditionally-compiled source code`.

:dp:`fls_pf1v89h7pjhh`
A :t:`construct` subject to :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg` where the related
:t:`configuration predicate` evaluates to ``false`` is not considered part of a
Rust program.

:dp:`fls_y56RGw3cbFex`
A :t:`crate root module` subject to :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg` where the related
:t:`configuration predicate` evaluates to ``false`` is considered empty except
for all :t:`[attribute]s` up to the invoked :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg`.

:dp:`fls_h6b1fuw4nvi1`
An :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg_attr` where the related :t:`configuration predicate`
evaluates to ``false`` is not considered part of a Rust program.


.. _fls_8JB3SJqamdpU:

Program Entry Point
-------------------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_dp64b08em9BJ`
A :dt:`program entry point` is a :t:`function` that is invoked at the start of
a Rust program.

:dp:`fls_sbGnkm8Ephiu`
A :dt:`main function signature` is the :t:`function signature` required for the
:t:`program entry point` function named ``main``.

* :dp:`fls_o4fxok23134r`
  It lacks :t:`[function qualifier]s` ``async`` and ``unsafe``,

* :dp:`fls_bk755pvc1l53`
  Its :t:`ABI` is Rust,

* :dp:`fls_a3je4wc53bmo`
  It lacks :t:`[generic parameter]s`,

* :dp:`fls_w8q15zp7kyl0`
  It lacks :t:`[function parameter]s`,

* :dp:`fls_4psnfphsgdek`
  It lacks a :t:`return type`,

* :dp:`fls_m7xfrhqif74`
  It lacks a :t:`where clause`,

* :dp:`fls_qq9fzrw4aykd`
  It has a :t:`function body`.
files.after:
.. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT OR Apache-2.0
   SPDX-FileCopyrightText: The Ferrocene Developers

.. default-domain:: spec

.. _fls_hdwwrsyunir:

Program Structure and Compilation
=================================

.. _fls_s35hob3i7lr:

Source Files
------------

.. rubric:: Syntax

.. syntax::

   SourceFile ::=
       ZeroWidthNoBreakSpace?
       Shebang?
       InnerAttributeOrDoc*
       Item*

   ZeroWidthNoBreakSpace ::=
       $$\u{FEFF}$$

   Shebang ::=
       $$#!$$ ~[NewLine]*

   NewLine ::=
       $$\n$$

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_4vicosdeaqmp`
A :dt:`source file` contains the program text consisting of :t:`[inner
attribute]s`, :t:`[inner doc comment]s`, and :t:`[item]s`. The location of a
:t:`source file` is tool defined.

:dp:`fls_ann3cha1xpek`
A :s:`Shebang` does not have an effect on the compilation.

.. _fls_e9hwvqsib5d5:

Modules
-------

.. rubric:: Syntax

.. syntax::

   ModuleDeclaration ::=
       $$unsafe$$? $$mod$$ Name ModuleSpecification

   ModuleSpecification ::=
       InlineModuleSpecification
     | OutlineModuleSpecification

   InlineModuleSpecification ::=
       $${$$
         InnerAttributeOrDoc*
         Item*
       $$}$$

   OutlineModuleSpecification ::=
       $$;$$

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_odd1hj3y1mgu`
A :dt:`module` is a container for zero or more :t:`[item]s`.

:dp:`fls_whgv72emrm47`
The ``unsafe`` :t:`keyword` of a :t:`module` is rejected, but may still
be consumed by :t:`[macro]s`.

:dp:`fls_qypjjpcf8uwq`
An :dt:`inline module` is a :t:`module` with an :s:`InlineModuleSpecification`.

:dp:`fls_cavwpr1ybk37`
An :dt:`outline module` is a :t:`module` with an :s:`OutlineModuleSpecification`.

:dp:`fls_plepew2319g4`
An :t:`outline module` loads a :t:`source file` and considers the text of the
:t:`source file` to be inlined within the context of the :t:`outline module`.

:dp:`fls_1aruwps62c4p`
The location of a :t:`module` :t:`source file` can be specified using
:t:`attribute` :c:`path`.

.. rubric:: Examples

.. code-block:: rust

   #[path = "path/to/module"]
   pub mod module {
   	#![allow(dead_code)]

        struct Struct;
        pub mod other;
   }

.. _fls_maw4u1o8q37u:

Crates
------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_qwghk79ok5h0`
A :t:`crate` is a unit of compilation and linking that contains a tree of
nested :t:`[module]s`.

:dp:`fls_unxalgMqIr3v`
The :dt:`crate type` of a :t:`crate` is the value of the :t:`attribute`
``crate_type`` of a :t:`crate` or the value of ``--crate-type`` flag passed to
the tool compiling the :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_e7jGvXvTsFpC`
The :t:`crate type` of a :t:`crate` if not specified is ``bin``.

:dp:`fls_kQiJPwb2Hjcc`
A :t:`crate` may be subject to multiple :t:`[crate type]s`, treating each type
as a separate :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_9ub6ks8qrang`
A :t:`binary crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``bin``.

:dp:`fls_OyFwBtDGVimT`
A :t:`binary crate` that is not subject to :t:`attribute` ``no_main`` shall have
a :t:`function` in scope of its :t:`crate root module` under the :t:`name`
``main`` with a :t:`main function signature`.

:dp:`fls_jQqXxPyND1ds`
The :t:`function` in scope of a :t:`binary crate`'s :t:`crate root module` under
the :t:`name` ``main`` with a :t:`main function signature` is the :t:`binary
crate`'s :t:`program entry point`.

:dp:`fls_d9nn4yuiw1ja`
A :dt:`library crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``lib``, ``rlib``,
``staticlib``, ``dylib``, or ``cdylib``.

:dp:`fls_Mf62VqAhoZ3c`
A :dt:`proc-macro crate` is a :t:`crate` whose :t:`crate type` is ``proc-macro``.

:dp:`fls_RJJmN4tP7j4m`
A :t:`proc-macro crate` shall not declare :t:`[item]s` in its :t:`crate root
module` with  :t:`public visibility` unless the :t:`item` is a :t:`procedural
macro`.

:dp:`fls_h93C3wfbAoz1`
Only a :t:`proc-macro crate` shall declare :t:`[procedural macro]s`.

.. _fls_gklst7joeo33:

Crate Imports
-------------

.. rubric:: Syntax

.. syntax::

   ExternalCrateImport ::=
       $$extern$$ $$crate$$ CrateIndication Renaming? $$;$$

   CrateIndication ::=
       Identifier
     | $$self$$

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_d0pa807s5d5h`
A :t:`crate import` specifies a required dependency on an external :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_vfam3wzeAiah`
A :dt:`crate indication` is a :t:`construct` that indicates a :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_2v7k7p4v0xra`
A :t:`renaming` provides an alternative :t:`name` for an existing name.

:dp:`fls_ft860vkz0lkc`
A :t:`crate import` binds an external :t:`crate` to its :t:`crate indication`.

:dp:`fls_k90qtnf8kgu1`
:t:`Crate indication` ``self`` shall require a :t:`renaming`.

:dp:`fls_siv8bl6s2ndu`
A :t:`crate import` with a :t:`renaming` with an :t:`identifier` binds the
external :t:`crate` to a local :t:`name` and introduces the local :t:`name` into
the enclosing :t:`scope`.

:dp:`fls_7vz5n3x6jo1s`
If a :t:`crate import` appears at the :t:`crate root module`, then the
:t:`crate indication` is added to the :t:`external prelude`.

:dp:`fls_3bgpc8m8yk4p`
A :t:`crate indication` shall resolve to an external :t:`crate`. The process of
resolving a :t:`crate indication` to an external :t:`crate` is tool-defined.

.. _fls_5w50kf83oo1u:

Compilation Roots
-----------------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_fhiqvgdamq5`
A :t:`crate root module` is the root of the nested :t:`module` tree of a
:t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_1NrcosNZJu5V`
A :dt:`crate root` is the :t:`crate root module` of a :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_tk8tl2e0a34`
A tool can define a :t:`crate root module` for a single :t:`crate`.

:dp:`fls_bsyfxdk3ap1t`
A :dt:`compilation root` is an input to a compilation performed by a tool. A
:t:`crate root module` is a :t:`compilation root`.

.. _fls_u1afezy1ye99:

Conditional Compilation
-----------------------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_9stc6nul6vq9`
:dt:`conditionally-compiled source code` is source code that may or may
not be considered a part of a Rust program depending on
:t:`[configuration predicate]s`.

:dp:`fls_a0u9nnaf6drz`
:t:`conditional compilation` is the process of compiling
:t:`conditionally-compiled source code`.

:dp:`fls_pf1v89h7pjhh`
A :t:`construct` subject to :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg` where the related
:t:`configuration predicate` evaluates to ``false`` is not considered part of a
Rust program.

:dp:`fls_y56RGw3cbFex`
A :t:`crate root module` subject to :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg` where the related
:t:`configuration predicate` evaluates to ``false`` is considered empty except
for all :t:`[attribute]s` up to the invoked :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg`.

:dp:`fls_h6b1fuw4nvi1`
An :t:`attribute` :c:`cfg_attr` where the related :t:`configuration predicate`
evaluates to ``false`` is not considered part of a Rust program.


.. _fls_8JB3SJqamdpU:

Program Entry Point
-------------------

.. rubric:: Legality Rules

:dp:`fls_dp64b08em9BJ`
A :dt:`program entry point` is a :t:`function` that is invoked at the start of
a Rust program.

:dp:`fls_sbGnkm8Ephiu`
A :dt:`main function signature` is the :t:`function signature` required for the
:t:`program entry point` function named ``main``.

* :dp:`fls_o4fxok23134r`
  It lacks :t:`[function qualifier]s` ``async`` and ``unsafe``,

* :dp:`fls_bk755pvc1l53`
  Its :t:`ABI` is Rust,

* :dp:`fls_a3je4wc53bmo`
  It lacks :t:`[generic parameter]s`,

* :dp:`fls_w8q15zp7kyl0`
  It lacks :t:`[function parameter]s`,

* :dp:`fls_4psnfphsgdek`
  It lacks a :t:`return type`,

* :dp:`fls_m7xfrhqif74`
  It lacks a :t:`where clause`,

* :dp:`fls_qq9fzrw4aykd`
  It has a :t:`function body`.
